title: Insert behavior basics
summary: Understand how INSERT statements handle NULLs, defaults, and required columns in the inventory_items table.

sketchBlocks.title: Why column lists matter in INSERT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When adding new rows with `INSERT`, it's safer to specify which columns you're providing values for. This avoids mistakes if the table structure changes or if some columns have defaults or allow NULLs. For example:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test)
VALUES ('Keychain', 'Accessories', 3.99, 'active', 0);
```

Here, columns like `notes` (nullable) and `last_updated` (has a default) will be filled automatically.

sketchBlocks.title: NULLs and defaults in action
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
If you leave out a column in your INSERT and it allows NULL or has a DEFAULT, SQL will use those. For example, omitting `notes` (nullable) and `last_updated` (default):

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test)
VALUES ('Sample Coaster', 'Kitchen', 2.50, 'draft', 1);
```

- `notes` becomes NULL
- `last_updated` becomes '2026-02-01' (the default)

But if you omit a NOT NULL column with no default, you'll get an error.

quizDraft.title: Insert with omitted nullable and default columns
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new inventory item named 'Desk Mat' in the 'Accessories' category, priced at 18.00, with status 'active' and is_test 0. Omit the `notes` and `last_updated` columns from your INSERT statement. Write the SQL query.
quizDraft.hint: Use an explicit column list and leave out `notes` and `last_updated`.

help.concept: When you omit nullable or defaulted columns in an INSERT, SQL fills them with NULL or the default value.
help.hint_1: List only the columns you want to provide values for. The database will use defaults or NULLs for the rest.
help.hint_2: Your column list should include name, category, price, status, and is_test only.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test)
VALUES ('Desk Mat', 'Accessories', 18.00, 'active', 0);

quizDraft.title: Insert with explicit NULL
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new inventory item named 'Sample Magnet' in the 'Decor' category, priced at 2.25, with status 'draft', is_test 1, and notes set to NULL. Let `last_updated` use its default. Write the SQL query.
quizDraft.hint: Include the `notes` column in your column list and set its value to NULL.

help.concept: You can explicitly insert NULL into columns that allow it by listing the column and using the NULL keyword.
help.hint_1: In your column list, include notes and set its value to NULL.
help.hint_2: Don't provide a value for last_updated; let the default apply.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes)
VALUES ('Sample Magnet', 'Decor', 2.25, 'draft', 1, NULL);

quizDraft.title: Error from missing required column
quizDraft.prompt: Try to insert a new inventory item with only the `name`, `category`, and `price` columns. What happens and why? Write an INSERT statement that would cause this error.
quizDraft.hint: Omitting NOT NULL columns with no default causes an error.

help.concept: If you omit a NOT NULL column that doesn't have a default, SQL will reject the insert.
help.hint_1: Try leaving out columns like status or is_test, which are NOT NULL and have no default.
help.hint_2: Write an INSERT that only includes name, category, and price. The database will complain about missing required columns.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price)
VALUES ('Test Item', 'Accessories', 5.00);

quizDraft.title: Which columns can be omitted safely?
quizDraft.prompt: Which columns in the inventory_items table can you safely omit from an INSERT statement and still have the row inserted successfully? (Choose all that apply.)
quizDraft.hint: Check which columns are nullable or have a default value.

help.concept: Columns that are nullable or have a default value can be omitted from an INSERT statement.
help.hint_1: Look for columns with DEFAULT or that allow NULL in the schema.
help.hint_2: Notes is nullable; last_updated and status have defaults; others may be required.

quizDraft.title: Default value for last_updated
quizDraft.prompt: When you omit the `last_updated` column in an INSERT, what value will it have by default?
quizDraft.hint: Check the table's schema for the default value.

help.concept: Columns with a DEFAULT clause will use that value if omitted in an INSERT.
help.hint_1: Look for the DEFAULT value for last_updated in the schema.
help.hint_2: It's a date string set in the CREATE TABLE statement.

quizDraft.template: If you omit last_updated in an INSERT, its value will be: ____
